zh.lifeSummary: Cyfnerth I ab Iddon I "Galed"是Iddon I ap Trahaearn I的兒子，於大約1130年出生在英國威爾斯波斯。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Cyfnerth I ab Iddon I "Galed"是Iddon I ap Trahaearn I的儿子，于大约1130年出生在英国威尔斯波斯。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: ab
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Long, Minh, Muoi, Phan, Phat, Vinh, Cuong, Hien, Hoa, Lam, Lan.  Chinese Wing, Chi, Kin, Cheuk, Chi Wai, Chuen, Hon, Keung, Wah, Wai, Chiu, Ho.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 敖, see   Ao  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Auerbach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Moshe, Emanuel, Isidor, Bronia, Chaim, Faigy, Menachem, Meyer, Yechezkel.
German: habitational name from any of several places in southern Germany and Austria called Auerbach ‘the stream at the water meadow’. See also 2,   Au  , and   Bach  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Auerbach in the Upper Palatinate, Bavaria. Compare   Averbach  .

variants.name: Ao
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 敖, meaning both ‘rambling’ and ‘deposed king without posthumous titles’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the personal name of Tai Ao (太敖), teacher of the legendary king Zhuanxu (traditional dates: 2514–2437 BC ). (ii) it is said that the surname is also borne by descendants of deposed kings of the former state of Chu, with no posthumous titles, during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

variants.name: Auer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Hans, Erwin, Matthias, Siegfried, Bernd, Bernhard, Erna, Franz, Fritz, Hermann, Horst.

German (mainly Bavaria): topographic name for someone living by a water meadow, German Au, or a habitational name for someone from a place called Au or Aue (see Au and Aue ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Czechia, and Slovenia.

nameMeanings.name: Iddon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: relationship name from the Middle English female personal name Ideyn, Idon, Idony (Old French Idone, Latin Idonea ‘suitable’). See also Ideson .

variants.name: Iden
meanings: English: habitational name from Iden Green in Benenden (Kent), recorded as Ydennec. 1200, or Iden Manor in Staplehurst (Kent) recorded as Idenne in 1313. The placenames derive from Old English īw ‘yew tree’ + denn ‘pasture’.
English: in Lancashire, a variant of Iddon, from the Middle English female personal name Ideyn, Idon, Idony (Old French Idone, Latin Idonea ‘suitable’).
North German: metronymic or patronymic from the personal name   Ide   2.

variants.name: Iddins
meanings: English: variant of Iddon with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare Ideson , Ison .

variants.name: Ideson
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Eddison  .   Redmonds, Dictionary of Yorks Surnames   notes that William Edeson also appears as Ideson, 1583–6 in the Rothwell Parish Registers, and that in Skipton Parish Registers Humphrey Edson or Eedson, whose son Humphrey was baptized in 1695, was the ancestor of a succession of Humphreys, whose surname appears as Ideson from 1724. The name is correctly pronounced /ɪdəsən/.  Compare  Iddison  .

2 English: (i) relationship name from the Middle English female personal name Ide + son. It may also have been used as a pet form of Idon(y), see (ii). (ii) relationship name, alternatively perhaps from the Middle English female personal name Idon(y), Ideyn ( see Iddon ) + son, with reduction of Idonson or Ideynson to Ideson. However, it is uncertain if either name in (i) and (ii) gave rise to a hereditary surname.

nameMeanings.name: "Galed"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmel, Agostino, Carlo, Carmelo, Giuseppe, Sal, Salvatore, Santo, Vincenzo.  Spanish Jesus, Jose, Nadina, Roberto.
Spanish (southwestern): from galea ‘galleon, (war)ship’, presumably a metonymic occupational name for a shipwright or a sailor.
Italian: habitational name from Galea in Calabria.

variants.name: Galera
meanings: Spanish:
habitational name from Galera, the name of several places in Spain.
variant of   Galea   ‘galleon, (war)ship’.

variants.name: Galeas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Carlos Alberto, Pablo, Santos, Absalon, Alvaro, Armando, Armondo, Blanca, Carlos Ernesto, Edgardo.
Spanish (southwestern): plural form of   Galea  . This surname is most common in Honduras.

en.lifeSummary: Cyfnerth I ab Iddon I "Galed" was born about 1130, in Powys, Wales, United Kingdom as the son of Iddon I ap Trahaearn I. He had at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Cyfnerth I ab Iddon I "Galed" est né environ 1130, à Powys, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, fils de Iddon I ap Trahaearn I. Il a eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Cyfnerth I ab Iddon I "Galed" wurde um 1130 in Powys, Wales, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Iddon I ap Trahaearn I geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Cyfnerth I ab Iddon I "Galed" è nato il circa 1130 nel Powys, Galles, Regno Unito come figlio di Iddon I ap Trahaearn I. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio.

ru.lifeSummary: Cyfnerth I ab Iddon I "Galed" родился около 1130 г. в местности поуис, Уэльс, Великобритания. Его отца звали Iddon I ap Trahaearn I. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cyfnerth I ab Iddon I "Galed" nació aproximadamente 1130, en Powys, Gales, Reino Unido como hijo de Iddon I ap Trahaearn I. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo.
